text.title: API Versioning Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-versioning work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Required field added in v1; existing clients fail validation; outage.
antipattern.root-cause: Team thought "new field" is always additive; missed that required tightens contract.
antipattern.fix: Use oasdiff classifier; require major bump on any field-required addition. See rules breaking-change-classifier + semver-bump-on-breaking.

antipattern.symptom: Some endpoints carry version in path, others in Accept header; partner SDK has to support both.
antipattern.fix: Pick one scheme in info.x-versioning; enforce via Spectral rule. See rule one-scheme-per-api.

antipattern.symptom: v1 deprecated "soon"; six months later still in use; team afraid to remove.
antipattern.root-cause: No date in Sunset header; verbal commitments.
antipattern.fix: Add Sunset header on deprecated endpoints with ISO date; broadcast 90+ days ahead. See rule deprecation-window-declared.

antipattern.symptom: Client sets `version: "v2"` in request body; CDN cannot cache; proxy cannot route.
antipattern.fix: Move version to URL path or Accept header; deprecate body flag. See rule no-version-in-payload.
